intro: Hey! I’m your Helper Squirrel — your guide through the website, the training app, classes, knots, climbing techniques, business lessons, and account setup.
If you're new, I can explain how the classes work, how the app works, and what you’ll learn in each part of the training system.
Want to know how much classes cost, what’s included, or how to prepare? Just ask me.
I can also break down knots, climbing techniques, business skills, and real‑world tree work situations.
Need help installing the app or setting up your account? I’ve got you covered.
I’m always here in the bottom‑right corner — click me anytime you need help.
Let’s get you climbing smarter, safer, and more confidently.
Ready when you are — what do you want to learn today?
website_info: This website is your full hub for professional tree climbing training. You can explore classes, learn about the training app, check gear, and get direct help from me anytime.
The site is designed to guide you from beginner to advanced climber. Everything is organized so you can grow step‑by‑step.
If you're preparing for a class, the website gives you early access to training materials so you can start learning before you even show up.
The Classes page shows pricing, what’s included, and how each session works.
The About page explains the training app and how it connects to your real‑world climbing progress.
The Gear page explains the equipment used in tree climbing and why each piece matters.
This website is your roadmap — the app is your training ground.
class_info: Group Classes cost $80 and cover gear setup, throwline work, basic climbing, movement, safety systems, and real‑tree practice.
Personal 1‑on‑1 sessions cost $200 per hour and let you focus on anything you want — advanced climbing, rigging, chainsaw technique, business coaching, or real‑world scenarios.
Classes are organized into hands‑on sections: gear setup, anchor setting, climbing systems, movement, safety, and canopy practice.
Group classes are great for beginners or anyone wanting structured training.
1‑on‑1 sessions are perfect for advanced climbers or people who want personalized coaching.
Every class includes real‑tree climbing time so you can apply what you learned immediately.
You can sign up for classes directly on the Classes page.
The training app prepares you before class so you show up confident and ready.
Completing app lessons unlocks discounts for live classes.
If you want help choosing a class, just ask me!
course_info: Courses inside the app are organized into categories: Knots, Climbing Techniques, Gear & Safety, Rigging, Cutting Techniques, and Business Skills.
Each course contains multiple short lessons designed to be easy to follow and packed with real‑world value.
You get lifetime access to every course you unlock.
The app tracks your progress automatically — no passwords needed.
Every 5 videos you complete unlocks a 30% discount on a live class.
Courses are designed to prepare you for real climbing, real jobs, and real safety situations.
You can learn at your own pace, anytime, anywhere.
Courses include step‑by‑step videos, explanations, and real‑world examples.
The app is your personal training library — always available, always growing.
If you want help choosing a course, I can guide you!
app_info: The training app gives you lifetime access to lessons, knots, climbing techniques, and business training.
You log in using your email — no password needed. The app sends you a secure login link.
Inside the app, you’ll find step‑by‑step videos that teach you everything from basic knots to advanced climbing systems.
The app includes a full knot library with real‑world explanations of when and why each knot is used.
You’ll learn climbing techniques like SRS, MRS, footlocking, rope walking, spur climbing, redirects, and limb walking.
The app also includes business lessons that teach you how to price jobs, communicate with customers, manage a crew, and run a professional tree service.
Every 5 videos you complete unlocks a 30% discount on a live class.
You can install the app on iPhone, Android, or desktop.
The app works offline after the first load — perfect for training outdoors.
Whether you're preparing for a class or learning on your own, the app gives you everything you need to climb safely and professionally.
account_setup: To set up your account, go to the App page and enter your email. You’ll receive a secure login link — no password needed.
Once logged in, the app automatically saves your progress, completed lessons, and unlocked rewards.
Your account gives you lifetime access to all lessons you’ve purchased or unlocked.
If you switch devices, just log in with the same email — your progress syncs instantly.
If you don’t receive your login link, check your spam folder or try again.
Your account stores your discounts, class unlocks, and any special rewards you earn.
You can update your email anytime by contacting support.
Your account is your key to the entire training system.
Once your account is set up, you can start learning immediately.
If you need help setting up your account, just ask me!
install_app: iPhone: Tap the share icon → Add to Home Screen.
Android: Tap the menu (⋮) → Install App.
Desktop: Click the install icon in the address bar.
Once installed, the app works offline after the first load.
The installed version loads faster and feels like a native app.
You can install the app on multiple devices — your progress syncs automatically.
If installation doesn’t appear, try refreshing the page.
The app is lightweight and optimized for speed.
Installing the app gives you a cleaner, distraction‑free training experience.
If you need help installing, I can walk you through it.
knots: Blake’s Hitch — smooth, reliable friction hitch used in MRS climbing. Great for beginners and pros.
Prusik — versatile hitch used for backups, rescues, and climbing systems.
Running Bowline — essential for rigging. Tightens under load and secures limbs safely.
Alpine Butterfly — creates a strong mid‑line loop for redirects or isolating rope damage.
Munter Hitch — used for controlled lowering and descent.
Bowline — strong, secure, and easy to untie after loading.
Clove Hitch — great for temporary holds and quick adjustments.
Figure‑Eight — used for climbing, anchoring, and creating secure loops.
Timber Hitch — perfect for securing logs and branches.
Double Fisherman’s — used to join two ropes securely.
climbing_techniques: SRS — efficient for long ascents and conserving energy.
MRS — classic arborist climbing, great for limb walking.
Rope Walking — smooth, efficient, low‑energy climbing.
Spur Climbing — used for removals and spar work.
Limb Walking — essential for pruning and canopy movement.
Redirects — change your rope angle for better positioning.
Canopy Anchoring — setting safe, strong anchor points.
Advanced Movement — combining redirects, limb walking, and body positioning.
business: Pricing Jobs — estimate based on time, risk, cleanup, and crew size.
Customer Communication — explain the job clearly and professionally.
Jobsite Flow — organize ropes, gear, and crew positions.
Crew Leadership — keep your team coordinated and safe.
Liability & Safety — protect yourself and your customers.
Real‑World Examples — storm damage, removals, pruning.
Marketing Basics — present your services professionally.
Upselling Services — offer additional work ethically.
Professionalism — stand out as a reliable arborist.
